q: The concept of the centrifugal force can be applied in ________ devices.
An attractive force exists between the sun and the earth in a direction ________ to the path of the earth.
The process that causes denser particles to settle to the bottom is called ________ principle.
In a banked curved road, the ________ component of the normal force provides the required centripetal force.
The centrifugal governor is a device that uses the advantage of the ________ force.
When whirling a body attached to a string, a ________ force is originated.
The upward thrust acts ________ to a flying aeroplane body.
When a car turns around a level curved path, the frictional force acts ________ to the direction of the motion of the car.
When a car moves in a circular path which is inclined to the horizontal, the reaction force of the road acts ________ to the car.
The normal force exerted by a banked road is ________ to the road.
The centrifugal force is an apparent or ________ force which is equal and opposite to the centripetal force.
In a banked curved road, the angle that the inclined track makes with the ________ is called the angle of banking.
An object that is accelerating must have a ________ acting on it.
When a car turns around a level curved path, the ________ force provides the required centripetal force.
When an automobile drives round a curve, it must have a force exerted towards the ________ of the curve.
The centrifugal force is caused by the effect of ________.
When a car turns in a circular path or a curve, a ________ force exists between the road and the car tyres.
For uniform circular motion, the centripetal acceleration vector points towards the ________ of the circular path.
The ________ action of a washing machine is used in spin drying the clothes.
When we swing a ball on the end of a string in a circle, the ________ in the string provides the required centripetal force.
In a laboratory centrifuge, the ________ acceleration causes the denser particles to settle to the bottom.
A centrifugal pump converts mechanical energy into ________ energy.
Removing the excess water by spin drying clothes in a washing machine is done with the help of ________ force.
The force that enables an object to keep it moving in the circle is the ________ force.
The banking angle of a road is chosen so that this condition holds for the ________ speed.
When a car makes a turn on an unbanked road, the ________ force acting upon the tyres of the car provides the required centripetal force for circular motion.
A ________ is a piece of equipment that puts an object in rotation around a fixed axis.
A satellite orbiting a planet is supported by the ________ force.
Banking of roads is raising the ________ edge of the curved road higher than the inner edge.
For uniform circular motion, the acceleration is ________ acceleration.
An object moving in a circle must have a ________ applied to it to keep it moving in the circle.
The ________ component of the normal force acts as the required centripetal force.
If the ________ force is not sufficient, the car going in the unbanked curved road will skid out of the circular path.
On an unbanked road, the ________ force is supplied by friction between the tyres and the road.
The curved road must be banked to provide the necessary ________ force to the automobiles.
The direction of the ________ acceleration is towards the centre of the circle.
When a car makes a turn on an unbanked road, the car will skid out of the circular path if the ________ force is not sufficient.
The upward thrust acting on a flying plane is also known as the ________ force.
The centripetal force is ________ to the direction of the motion of the body in circular motion.
When the aeroplane tilts, the horizontal component of the ________ force provides the required centripetal force.
When the aeroplane tilts, the horizontal component of the ________ force acts normally to the motion of the aeroplane.
When a car moves in a circular path which is inclined to the horizontal, the ________ component of the normal force acts as the required centripetal force.
When a car turns around a level curved path, the frictional force behaves as a ________ force.
When a car moves around a curve, the person in the car feels an ________ force exerted on him.
When a car moves in a curved circular path, the ________ component of the normal force is the required centripetal force.
The ________ force appears to act in a direction pointing away from the centre.
In a washing machine, the washed clothes are spun very fast and excess water is removed completely with the help of ________ force.
The centrifugal governor is a device used to measure and regulate the ________ of a machine.
The ________ force is the force acting towards the centre of the circular path around which the object is moving.
________ force is the force acting on an object in circular motion which is directed towards the axis of rotation or centre of curvature.
